payload.project_name: Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection Services for LIRR/MNR
payload.description: Five-year contract for FRA-mandated ultrasonic rail flaw and joint bar inspection services for LIRR and MNR, including ultrasonic testing, joint bar detection, and eddy current testing

evidence_refs.source_quote: Long Island Rail Road (“LIRR”), on behalf of itself and Metro-North Railroad (“MNR”), collectively (the “Railroads”), seeks MTA Board approval to award a public works contract to Sperry Rail, Inc. (“Sperry”) for Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection Services.

tool_args.label: Initial proposal amount and cost savings

payload.raw_value_text: Sperry submitted its initial proposal in the amount of $43,813,976. Through negotiations, LIRR was able to secure a final price of $41,298,581. This represents a cost avoidance of approximately 6 percent ($2,515,395)
payload.comparison_period: initial proposal vs negotiated final price

payload.subject: Funding source

evidence_refs.source_quote: Funding for this project is included in the LIRR and MNR Operating Budget.

payload.claim_text: Sperry, the incumbent, will provide FRA-mandated ultrasonic rail flaw and joint bar inspection services performed along the right-of-way throughout the Railroads' territories
payload.subject: Sperry is the incumbent vendor

evidence_refs.source_quote: Sperry, the incumbent, will provide Federal Railroad Administration (“FRA”)–mandated ultrasonic rail flaw and joint bar inspection services performed along the right-of-way throughout the Railroads’ territories.

payload.claim_text: Currently, the Agencies do not possess the resources to perform the services associated with this contract. Both agencies require this work, as rail flaw testing and joint bar inspection services are vital safety requirements
payload.subject: No alternatives available

evidence_refs.source_quote: None available. Currently, the Agencies do not possess the resources to perform the services associated with this contract. Both agencies require this work, as rail flaw testing and joint bar inspection services are vital safety requirements to both agencies in determining the strength, quality, and reliability of rails during their operation.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

payload.claim_text: The latest technologies in ultrasonic testing, joint bar detection, and eddy current testing will be used to identify and detect internal and external flaws and fractures in track and rail that may not be visible to the naked eye
payload.subject: Testing technologies

evidence_refs.source_quote: The latest technologies in ultrasonic testing, joint bar detection, and eddy current testing will be used to identify and detect internal and external flaws and fractures in track and rail that may not be visible to the naked eye.

Long Island Rail Road (“LIRR”), on behalf of itself and Metro-North Railroad (“MNR”), collectively (the “Railroads”), seeks MTA Board approval to award a public works contract to Sperry Rail, Inc.
on behalf of itself and Metro-North Railroad (“MNR”)

Sperry Rail, Inc. 5 years Contract # 397260RFP $41,298,581 (est.) Staff Summary Attached Award of a contract solicited through a Request for Proposal for Rail Flaw Testing and Joint Bar Inspection Services.
The estimated quantity award amount is $41,298,581 (LIRR $17,020,961 / MNR $24,277,620).
A Request for Expression of Interest (“RFEI”) was first conducted on April 12, 2022
LIRR issued the RFP, and again performed an extensive outreach to the track maintenance industry and published the advertisement for the procurement of these services on January 11, 2023
On April 10, 2023, proposals were received from two vendors: Sperry and Pro-Traxx.
On May 1, 2023, the MTA Selection Committee ("SC"), comprised of representatives from LIRR and MNR Maintenance of Way user groups, evaluated the technical proposals

Subject Request for Authorization to Award Various Procurements Department LIRR Procurement Department Head Name Jacqueline Waddell Department Head Signature Project Manager Name Rose Davis Board Action Order To Date Approval Info Other 1 Committee 04/29/2024 2 Board 04/30/2024
The MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights ("DDCR") has established a zero percent DBE goal for this contract.
Funding for this project is included in the LIRR and MNR Operating Budget.
Sperry, the incumbent, will provide Federal Railroad Administration (“FRA”)–mandated ultrasonic rail flaw and joint bar inspection services
The estimated quantity award amount is $41,298,581 (LIRR $17,020,961 / MNR $24,277,620).

Sperry submitted its initial proposal in the amount of $43,813,976. Through negotiations, LIRR was able to secure a final price of $41,298,581. This represents a cost avoidance of approximately 6 percent ($2,515,395)
a cost avoidance of approximately 6 percent
